digests.summary: DeepSeek V4 drops as the biggest open-source model ever, OpenAI's GPT Images 2.0 cracks multilingual typography, and April 2026 closes out the most packed month for LLM releases in AI history.

A humanoid robot outruns every human in history at the Beijing Half-Marathon, OpenAI kills Sora on April 26, GPT-5.5 launches with autonomous OS control, and Alibaba's Wan 2.7 challenges GPT Image 2 on typography with 50 million font training samples.

Claude Mythos stays locked — offensive cybersecurity capabilities deemed too dangerous; Grok 4.3's 4-agent council rewrites inference-time architecture; humanoid robots make Bloomberg's cover; NVIDIA ships Nemotron 3 Nano Omni open omni model at 9× efficiency.

Anthropic launches Claude Design to rival Figma and Adobe; Meta acquires Assured Robot Intelligence for humanoid AI; OpenAI-Microsoft end Azure exclusivity; Grok 4.3 enters the AA Intelligence top 10.

Claude Opus 4.7 sets a new SWE-bench record at 87.6% as Anthropic embeds AI natively into Adobe CC, Blender, and SketchUp via Claude Connectors — while Monotype's natural-language font search and Figma's MCP server signal a new era of AI-native design tooling.

Self-evolving AI breaks into the mainstream: AlphaEvolve discovers better algorithms across DNA sequencing and quantum circuits, GPT-5.5 Instant cuts hallucinations 52% as ChatGPT's new default, Grok 4.3 enters the top-10 leaderboard, and Monotype AI Search transforms how designers find fonts.

IBM's MAMMAL biology foundation model outperforms AlphaFold 3 on antibody design; SubQ launches the first subquadratic LLM with a 12M-token context; Mercury 2 hits 1,000 tokens/sec via diffusion; Claude Design + Canva AI 2.0 redraw the creative toolchain.

Anthropic surpasses OpenAI in enterprise adoption for the first time; Google Antigravity IDE ships A2A + AgentKit 2.0; Unitree reveals a $650K rideable mecha; and ChatGPT gains bank-account-connected personal finance.

Claude Opus 4.8 claims the global #1 intelligence rank hours after Anthropic raises $65B at a $965B valuation; Google I/O delivers Gemini Omni (any-input-to-video), Gemini 3.5 Flash (81% SWE-bench), and Figma's native canvas Design Agent; and Google Co-scientist becomes the first AI hypothesis engine peer-reviewed in Nature.

NVIDIA Cosmos 3 sweeps every open-source leaderboard (T2I, I2V, robot policy) with 8,680 HuggingFace stars on debut; Microsoft Build 2026 ships a family of 7 in-house MAI models plus a 1-petaflop dev box; Anthropic confidentially files S-1 for a potential trillion-dollar IPO; and MiniMax M3 lands with 1M-context native multimodality.

OpenAI previews GPT-5.6 Sol under a US government-vetted access lock while Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 remains suspended; Krea 2 ships as the most uncensored open-source image model with ComfyUI workflows; Figma Config 2026 and Adobe Firefly agentic studio redefine the design stack; and Figure AI hits one robot per hour at BotQ.

Frontier model intelligence races accelerate alongside transformative shifts in collaborative design workflows, hybrid retrieval systems, and enterprise-grade robotics, marking a definitive pivot toward scalable, real-world AI deployment.

Claude Fable 5 leads intelligence rankings and the Text Arena as Grok launches agentic testing, deep context windows mature, open-source typography gains traction, Figure AI scales robot production, and researchers shift from parameter scaling to architectural efficiency.

Model performance gaps close as pricing compresses and retrieval stacks mature, while agentic workflows, open-weight image generation, and humanoid testing push toward mainstream production deployment.

Claude Fable 5 extends its intelligence lead as new latency benchmarks and hybrid RAG stacks reshape AI workflows, while breakthroughs in agentic reasoning, collaborative design interfaces, and humanoid robotics push autonomous systems closer to production deployment.

DeepSeek scales speculative decoding infrastructure while Karpathy maps LLM Wiki retrieval layers, as Apollo 2 advances robotic training facilities and multilingual typography reaches production deployment.

Fable 5 holds its lead in new benchmarks as intelligence gaps compress across the frontier, while breakthroughs in speculative decoding, autonomous research pipelines, and tighter agentic workflows redefine how teams measure and deploy AI systems.

Frontier benchmarks converge as Anthropic and GPT lead rankings while scalable agent orchestration, structured retrieval tools, and advanced robotics push practical AI into mainstream development and creative workflows.

Claude Fable 5 and Meta Llama 4 Scout lead current intelligence rankings while performance benchmarks now prioritize output token throughput over context size to bypass initial latency delays, commercial models adopt segmented pricing tiers, and robotics research advances through computational soft-body frameworks paired with hybrid vector databases for reliable retrieval.

Today AI development centers on practical agent tooling and infrastructure tuning, as new compact SWE benchmarks launch alongside open-weights fine-tuning pipelines, latency bottlenecks shift toward output generation, and hybrid retrieval architectures gain traction over standalone vector databases.

Market benchmarks tighten as Claude Fable 5 solidifies its intelligence lead while GPT-5.6 Sol captures a top-three spot amid shifting index dynamics. Performance standards shift toward sub-30 millisecond latency, driven by speculative decoding optimizations and Grok 4.5's launch for advanced reasoning tasks. Development ecosystems pivot toward refined prompt architectures and knowledge base routing to streamline personalized AI operations.
